title: PMAnalysis
description: Output of Agent: project-manager. An on-demand analytical artifact (NOT a periodic status report — that is status-update-author). Synthesizes scope/cost/schedule/risk into an exec-ready piece (situation, analysis, options, recommendation, risks, next_steps) with per-claim citations and explicit confidence/escalation flags. Extends pm_artifact_base.

project_id.description: Project key (e.g. 'QPB1'). Drives Drive folder routing and search index partition.

question.description: Verbatim copy of the question / ask the analysis answers. Mirrors the input so downstream consumers can route on it.

depth.description: Analytical depth: quick = 1–2 page bullet brief; detailed = full analysis with options & next steps; formal_memo = exec-ready memo with executive-summary tone.

audience.description: Tone & lane. 'owner' forces tier-3 review (Charles approves before any owner-facing copy moves).

constraints.description: Optional verbatim copy of any constraints from the input (timebox, budget, page limit, regulatory).

data_window.description: Inclusive ISO-date window the analysis pulled data over.

situation.description: Plain statement of the current situation the analysis starts from. Facts only, with citations. No opinions, no recommendations.

analysis.description: Reasoning showing how the situation was decomposed: drivers, constraints, dependencies, tradeoffs, calculations. Reasoning is shown, not summarized away.

options.description: Decision options considered. At least one option is required even when the recommendation is to do nothing (label that option 'No action'). Options are ranked; rank 1 is the recommended option.

recommendation.description: The recommended option and the reasoning, in plain English. References the option label from options[] explicitly. Avoids hedging fluff. If recommendation cannot be made (insufficient data, conflicting constraints), state that and add 'low_confidence' or 'missing_data' to escalation_reasons.

risks.description: Risks introduced by, or surfaced during, the analysis. Each entry has severity, likelihood, mitigation, and an owner role.

next_steps.description: Concrete actions to advance the recommendation. Each entry has an action, owner role, due date, and dependencies.

claim_confidence.description: Per-claim confidence overrides. Use when individual sections (e.g. Cost) are materially less confident than the artifact-level confidence. Each entry tags the section and a 0.0-1.0 score.

label.description: Short label for the option (e.g. 'Accept the slip', 'Crash the schedule with a second crew', 'No action').

impact_summary.description: Plain-English summary of the impact (cost, schedule, risk, scope) of taking this option.

estimated_cost_impact.description: Optional structured cost impact. If unknown, use null and explain in impact_summary; do not fabricate dollar figures.

amount_usd.description: Signed USD impact. Negative = savings vs baseline; positive = additional cost.

is_estimate.description: True when the figure is a rough order of magnitude rather than a tied-back number.

estimated_schedule_impact_days.description: Signed days of schedule impact. Negative = ahead of baseline / pulls in; positive = pushes out. Null when the option has no schedule impact or it cannot be honestly estimated.

recommendation_rank.description: 1 = recommended option. 2..N = alternatives in order of preference. Ties are not allowed; if two options are genuinely equivalent, surface that under 'recommendation' and pick a rank.

mitigation.description: Concrete mitigation plan; must be actionable, not aspirational ('monitor closely' alone is not a mitigation).

owner_role.description: Role label (not a person's name unless the input explicitly named them) responsible for the mitigation.

action.description: Imperative-mood action ('Issue PCO for chiller substitution to owner', 'Lock 2-week mobilization buffer in DH-2 sequence B').

due_date.description: Inclusive ISO date when the action is due. Null when the action is gated on another step.

dependencies.description: Other actions or artifact IDs this action depends on. Plain-English labels are fine.
